meta.purpose: LLM-ready website build spec for Hiten Raj Singh's Expedition Portfolio
meta.theme: Adventure expedition — Base Camp to Summit
meta.philosophy: Human-first, cinematic, restraint over complexity. Feels crafted by a real person, not generated by AI.

owner.name: Hiten Raj Singh
owner.headline: Pre-Final Year CSE Student @ MIT Manipal | Software Engineer | Hackathon Finalist | Builder

modes.day: Warm amber light, clear mountain sky, standard colors
modes.night: Deep indigo sky, aurora visible (faint greens and purples behind mountains), toggle class .night-mode on <body> swaps CSS custom properties

tech_stack_build.framework: Next.js (React) or plain HTML/CSS/JS — both work for every section
tech_stack_build.smooth_scroll: Lenis — one import, five lines setup
tech_stack_build.animations_hero: GSAP + ScrollTrigger — ONLY for 3 hero transitions
tech_stack_build.theming: CSS Custom Properties — one .night-mode class on body swaps all colors
tech_stack_build.audio_optional: Howler.js — single global ambient toggle, can be omitted entirely
tech_stack_build.rule: No WebGL, no canvas, no Three.js. Everything done via CSS + SVG + minimal vanilla JS.

scroll_and_transitions.system: Lenis smooth scroll on desktop; standard vertical scroll on mobile

sidebar_map.type: Fixed right-side element, 7 dots on vertical line
sidebar_map.behavior: Active dot glows on current section; dot click scrolls to section; line fills top-to-bottom as user progresses
sidebar_map.implementation: Pure CSS + one scroll event listener

transition_list.description: Fog clears, sky darkens, aurora emerges. CSS opacity + blur on scroll threshold. ~20 lines.
Camera tips forward, powder snow rushes upward, slope animates. CSS + GSAP timeline. First wow moment.
Wave builds and breaks across viewport revealing Pipeline scene. CSS keyframes on SVG wave paths.
Sky rushes upward, clouds part. CSS translateY on background layers + speed blur.
Freefall slows, light shifts from sky-blue to warm amber (or aurora purple in Night mode). Fade + CSS color transition.
Pages rustle closed. Deep water rumble fades in. Sound + cross-fade between background layers.
Rivers converge to calm. Scene rises to mountain panorama. Elevation cross-fade.

expedition_log.description: Fixed bottom-left element. One line shown at a time, changes on section entry. Written in Hiten's voice — short, specific, human.
expedition_log.implementation: One fixed div, text updated via IntersectionObserver. Fade in/out transition. Under 20 lines of JS.

lines.base_camp: Altitude: 5,364m. Visibility: uncertain. Base Camp established. JEE cleared. Now we climb.
lines.aurora: Paused everything for a moment just to ask: what am I actually building? Worth the stop.
lines.zermatt_tech: The red runs separate the people who know the syntax from the people who know the system.
lines.pipeline_projects: Eight projects shipped. Three survived real users. That's the only metric that matters.
lines.interlaken_achievements: At 4,200m the world gets very quiet. Top 130 of 15,000 teams felt like that.
lines.library_education: 8.04 CGPA and a lot of late-night debugging. Manipal taught me more outside the classroom.
lines.victoria_falls_leadership: 200+ trekkers, 3 donation drives, one fest with 900 attendees. Logistics is underrated.
lines.summit_contact: Kala Patthar. 5,643m. You scrolled all the way here — let's build something.

sections.scene: Fog-shrouded mountain base at dawn. Silhouettes of peaks visible above mist. Warm lantern light from tents.

animations.fog: CSS layered opacity + blur animation, slow drift
animations.title: Stamp reveal effect on load (scale + opacity)

sections.expedition_location: Above the Arctic Circle — the quiet before the journey
sections.scene: Full-viewport aurora sky. Mountain silhouettes below. No UI clutter. Just sky and stillness.
sections.purpose: Human beat — who Hiten is, in his own voice. No bullet points. No LinkedIn summary.

content.bio_short: Computer Science undergraduate at MIT Manipal. I build things for a living and break them for fun. My best code — like the best route up a mountain — feels obvious once you've found it.
content.bio_long: I enjoy transforming ideas into practical software through engineering, problem solving, and product thinking. My interests span full-stack development, AI applications, cloud technologies, and user-focused digital experiences. Beyond code, I lead volunteer drives, plan treks for 200+ people, and show up for communities I care about.
content.display_text: I build things for a living and break them for fun. My best code — like the best route up a mountain — feels obvious once you've found it.

animations.aurora: Two radial-gradient CSS bands animating with @keyframes translate + opacity. Opacity 0.18–0.28. Two layers at slightly different speeds for natural feel.
animations.text: Fade-in on scroll via Intersection Observer
animations.day_mode: Clear pre-dawn sky with fading stars

sections.copy_note: 2–3 sentences max. No heading. No CTAs. The scroll itself moves the visitor on.

sections.expedition_location: The Slopes of Zermatt — Swiss Alps
sections.scene: Illustrated piste map — hand-drawn mountain with colour-coded ski runs. Green = fundamentals, blue = intermediate, red/black = advanced.
sections.purpose: Skills showcase as a resort piste map
sections.weather_hud: Zermatt: Conditions: Clear. Temp: −6°C. Visibility: Excellent.
sections.hover_card_style: Resort notice board — skill name, level, projects it was used in

animations.paths: CSS hover on SVG runs — highlight + stroke-dashoffset reveal
animations.tooltip: Appears on hover, styled as resort board

sections.difficulty: ★★☆☆☆ — SVG piste map is main effort

sections.scene: Ocean horizon. Incoming waves — each wave is one project. Wave height suggests project scale.
sections.purpose: Projects showcase as a surfer's logbook

projects.name: RedXOfficial
projects.description: Official website for Red-X, MIT Manipal's socio-adventure club.
projects.impact: 400+ users in first month, 950+ page views. Improved club visibility and engagement.

projects.name: VVARSHAA
projects.description: Interactive tribute website built from 55K+ WhatsApp messages — behavioral analytics, leaderboards, quote extraction, and custom visualizations.
projects.impact: Converted raw group chat history into interactive storytelling and analytics for an 8-member friend group.

projects.name: VoicePay
projects.description: Voice-first payment platform making digital payments accessible through speech — ASR, NLU, multilingual TTS, OTP workflow.
projects.impact: Fully voice-controlled payment experience focused on users with visual, motor, or cognitive disabilities.

projects.name: BounceBack
projects.description: Behavior-based risk detection platform predicting churn and user disengagement with automated SMS retention alerts.
projects.impact: Analyzed 1000+ user behavior patterns. Generated automated retention alerts via neural network model.

projects.name: CURA
projects.description: Full-stack hospital management platform — 7 modules, 20+ views, patient/doctor management, invoice generation, billing workflows.

projects.name: InsaafBot
projects.description: AI legal assistant trained on 75+ years of historical legal archives — natural language legal search, 400+ scraped webpages.
projects.impact: Handled law-related queries spanning 75+ years. Trained on 400+ scraped webpages.

projects.name: BookEase
projects.description: Railway booking and management app with 7-table ER model, triggers, procedures, full CRUD workflow.

projects.name: MediTrack
projects.description: JavaFX desktop healthcare app — 10+ screens, 3+ hospital modules, patient/doctor/appointment management.

sections.card_style: Surfer's logbook page — slight paper texture (CSS box-shadow + border-radius), monospace labels, handwritten-style date
sections.scroll: Horizontal on desktop (Lenis), vertical on mobile

animations.waves: SVG wave paths with CSS keyframe animation
animations.card_reveal: Wave breaks on scroll threshold — opacity + translateY on card

sections.expedition_location: Interlaken Skydive — Switzerland, milestones at altitude
sections.scene: Multi-layer sky: thin cloud at top (national/international), mid-altitude (university), lower cloud (local/club). You fall through.
sections.card_style: CSS flip card — front: achievement name + level badge; back: full detail. CSS 3D transform.

animations.parallax: Layered CSS backgrounds at different scroll speeds — pure CSS transform
animations.card_reveal: Achievements appear as you scroll down through altitude
animations.freeze: Hovering an achievement freezes the fall animation and opens certificate popup

sections.expedition_location: The Ancient Library — somewhere between Nalanda and Oxford
sections.scene: Deep bookshelf across viewport. Stone arches. Warm amber light (day) or aurora through skylights (night).
sections.purpose: Education as library books — click a spine to open the book

books.degree: Bachelor of Technology — Computer Science Engineering
highlights: Coursework: DSA, DBMS, OS, Networks, Cryptography, SDLC

animations.book_click: CSS 3D transform — book slides out (translateZ), rotates open (rotateY perspective). Standard pattern.
animations.aurora_night: Two aurora bands as CSS overlay on upper third, filtered through skylight shapes
animations.skylight_day: Warm amber light gradient overlay — same implementation, two moods

sections.stillness_note: No background animation by design. Stillness IS the design choice here.

sections.scene: Multiple streams of water converging and falling together. Each stream is one leadership role.
sections.purpose: Leadership & extracurriculars as converging river streams

streams.label: Red-X Social Welfare
streams.impact: Led 3+ donation drives across NGOs and rehabilitation centers. Managed 3+ large-scale treks for 200+ participants — logistics, marketing, safety.

streams.label: Revels Core
streams.impact: Managed logistics and publicity for 3 events involving 900+ participants. Coordinated cross-functional teams for flagship fest.

streams.label: Placement Team
streams.impact: Organized 6+ pre-placement talks for leading companies. Managed operations for audiences of 250–300+ students.

streams.label: Red-X Management
streams.impact: Led week-long membership drive. Trained volunteers in pitching — 150+ membership sales.

streams.label: Revels Publicity
streams.impact: Led publicity and creative campaigns reaching 12,000+ students.

streams.label: TechTatva
streams.impact: Designed and hosted a debugging workshop for 100+ students.

streams.label: IECSE
streams.impact: Contributed to technical events, operations, and community initiatives.

streams.label: MIST Cybersecurity
streams.impact: Participated in cybersecurity-focused events, workshops, and technical initiatives.

streams.label: Photography Club
streams.impact: Member of the photography community at Manipal.

sections.click_behavior: Clicking a stream pauses the animation and opens a field journal card: role, org, duration, impact line
sections.card_style: Field journal — slide-in panel from right, styled as worn notebook page. CSS translateX transition.

animations.streams: CSS keyframe on SVG water paths — stroke-dashoffset + opacity pulses
animations.mist: CSS blur + opacity gradient at base. Static, no particles.

sections.scene: Wide mountain panorama at golden hour. Night mode: summit under aurora sky with star field.
sections.purpose: Contact form styled as a summit register. Social links as trail signs. Resume as an expedition permit.

form.placeholder_email: How to reach you at base camp

resume_button.label: Download Expedition Permit
resume_button.style: Stamped government permit document — CSS only

sections.on_submit: Prayer flag confetti — CSS confetti with SVG flag shapes (small rectangles on strings, keyframe scatter). ~30 lines JS.
sections.stillness_note: Section is quiet. Panorama breathes slowly. Form is the only interaction.

animations.panorama: CSS layered mountain silhouettes, slow parallax breathe
animations.night_stars: Subtle CSS star field in Night mode
animations.confetti: Prayer flag confetti on form submit

expedition_sidebar.behavior: Active dot glows; fill line grows top-to-bottom; dot click scrolls to section; hover shows section name tooltip
expedition_sidebar.implementation: Pure CSS + one scroll event listener; hidden on mobile

expedition_log.description: Fixed bottom-left corner. One line at a time, updates on section entry.
expedition_log.implementation: One fixed div + IntersectionObserver + fade transition. Under 20 lines JS.

day_night_toggle.behavior: Toggles .night-mode on <body>; all scene changes via CSS custom properties only

ambient_audio.day_track: Mountain wind, distant birdsong, gentle water
ambient_audio.night_track: Silence with occasional wind, deep low hum
ambient_audio.note: Site works perfectly without audio. Remove entirely if it feels like too much.

build_phases.title: Foundation
deliverables: Project setup — HTML/CSS/JS or Next.js scaffold
Google Fonts loaded (Playfair Display, Inter, IBM Plex Mono)
CSS custom properties for Day/Night
Lenis smooth scroll
Base Camp hero — fog animation, stamp text, compass CTA
Expedition sidebar — 7 dots, fill line, active state, hover labels
Expedition log element — fixed position, placeholder text, fade
Day/Night toggle wired up
Aurora CSS layer working in Night mode

build_phases.title: Sections
deliverables: Aurora Moment — CSS aurora, fade-in text
Zermatt Tech Stack — SVG piste map + hover cards
Pipeline Projects — SVG waves + horizontal scroll + logbook cards (most effort)
Interlaken Achievements — CSS parallax cloud layers + certificate flip cards
Library Education — bookshelf scene + CSS 3D book flip + aurora skylight overlay
Victoria Falls Leadership — SVG stream animation + field journal panel
Summit Contact — panorama + summit register form + prayer flag confetti

build_phases.title: Transitions
deliverables: Hero transition 1 — Ski slope (GSAP)
Hero transition 2 — Wave break (CSS + SVG)
Hero transition 3 — Freefall (CSS layers)
Atmospheric fades for remaining 4 transitions
Wire all expedition log lines to section changes

build_phases.title: Polish & Launch
deliverables: Write expedition log lines in Hiten's actual voice (already provided above)
@media (prefers-reduced-motion) variants for all animations
Mobile testing — all content accessible without transitions
Lighthouse ≥ 90 on mobile — lazy-load backgrounds, compress SVGs
SEO — meta tags, OG image (Base Camp scene at golden hour), page title
User test with 3 people, fix where they stop

design_rules.core_rule: If something is hard to implement, simplify it. One CSS animation done with care beats three WebGL scenes done in a rush.
no_list: Perfectly symmetrical layouts with identical card heights — expedition journals are messy
Stock-photo style or AI-generated landscape art
Too much animation everywhere at once — silence and stillness are part of the design
Corporate copy — 'results-driven developer' is a red flag
WebGL, Three.js, canvas (unless you already know it)
Per-section audio (replaced with one global toggle)
yes_list: Write in your own voice throughout
Let some sections be still — Library and Summit are quiet by design
Rough edges have soul — one hand-drawn SVG element beats a hundred perfect ones
Three transitions done beautifully over six done adequately
Restraint is a design choice, not a failure
